predecessor_cycles.omega_runner_3: structural assessment — identified 'no shell' as primary distroless blocker
predecessor_cycles.omega_runner_4: POC built distroless image at 95.1 MB (-70 MB vs production 165 MB), 4/5 smoke PASS, B3 BLOCKED (hexa CLI shells out via popen('sh -c …') needing GNU coreutils — dash alone insufficient)

goal: Unblock B3 by adding busybox-static (single static ELF + applet symlinks) to provide dirname/basename/printf/cat/awk/grep/sed/date/etc., then re-smoke; recommend production cutover if green.

busybox_addition.copy_pattern: Stage applets in builder under /opt/bb/{bin,usr/bin}, then two wholesale COPYs into runtime: '/opt/bb/bin/ -> /bin/' and '/opt/bb/usr/bin/ -> /usr/bin/'. Single busybox ELF + 42 explicit applet symlinks; remaining 227 applets reachable by busybox argv0 dispatch via 'busybox <applet>'.
busybox_addition.rationale: Distroless lacks /bin/sh — RUN cannot be used in shell-form on the runtime stage. Staging the symlink farm in the builder (where /bin/sh exists) keeps the runtime stage purely declarative (COPY only). Single static ELF means no library deps to chase.

smoke_c_busybox_sh_coreutils.stdout_excerpt: sh-works / /a/b / c / printf-works / Sun Apr 26 07:18:12 UTC 2026

smoke_e_hexa_run_println.note: This was the BLOCKED test in omega-runner-4. busybox now satisfies the popen('sh -c …') path used by resolve_interp() and dozens of other CLI sites.

smoke_f_hexa_run_exec_date.note: Run did not crash; underlying 'date' (busybox applet) invoked successfully. The 'void' literal in stdout is a hexa CLI exec() return-value semantic (separate from the runtime-image concern) — not a busybox/distroless regression.

observations: Build was clean — busybox-static apt add did not pull additional dependencies (statically linked).
Two-COPY pattern (/opt/bb/bin -> /bin, /opt/bb/usr/bin -> /usr/bin) is idempotent re-runnable and does not collide with distroless's empty /bin and /usr/bin.
tini still works (PID-1 reaping), curl/wget still work (TLS), hexa CLI now able to spawn child processes via popen.
Image growth from busybox is 2.4 MB (busybox-static binary itself ~1 MB + dpkg metadata + 42 hardlink-equivalents). Well below the 50 MB savings threshold.

reasoning: All 6/6 smoke tests PASS, including the previously BLOCKED B3 test (smoke-e: 'hexa run' with println).
Image savings of -67.5 MB (-40.9%) materially exceeds the 50 MB threshold called out in the runner-5 plan.
No new blockers surfaced; busybox has 227 additional applets available via argv0 dispatch should the CLI need anything beyond the 42 explicit symlinks.
Distroless attack surface reduction (no apt, no package manager, no GNU coreutils binaries beyond what busybox provides) is a security win on top of the size win.
Production Dockerfile change is mechanical: copy the validated Dockerfile.distroless layer pattern into the production Dockerfile (or rename + replace) and re-tag CI.
next_cycle_scope: omega-runner-6: replace docker/runner/Dockerfile with the validated distroless+busybox layout.
Run full hexa CI smoke + nexus bridge smoke (curl/wget paths) against the cutover image.
Tag a production image and update any Compose/k8s manifests pointing at hexa-runner:latest.
Add a regression test that asserts /bin/sh + dirname + basename are present (guards against accidental busybox removal).
cutover_recommendation_for_runner_6.rollback_for_cutover: Revert the production Dockerfile change; previous image (hexa-runner:cab8ef20 / 4899225b8c50) remains pullable for instant rollback.

rollback.instructions: git checkout -- /Users/ghost/core/hexa-lang/docker/runner/Dockerfile.distroless restores the runner-4 (dash-only) state. docker image rm hexa-runner-distroless:test-bb removes the new image. No production artifacts were touched in this cycle.

constraints_honored.hexa_only_session_dockerfile_is_infra_ex: Yes — Dockerfile.distroless is the prototype file already created in runner-4 for this exact purpose; per session note, infra changes confined to that POC file are allowed.
